data.title: KalVista prices upsized offering of $125M of 3.250% notes due 2031
data.description:
• KalVista Pharmaceuticals (NASDAQ:KALV) announced on Wednesday the pricing of its offering of $125M aggregate principal amount of 3.250% Convertible Senior Notes due 2031 in a private placement. 
  • The aggregate principal amount of the offering was increased from the previously announced offering size of $110M. 
  • The notes will be senior, unsecured obligations of KalVista and will bear interest at a rate of 3.250% per year, payable semi-annually in arrears on April 1 and October 1 of each year, beginning on October 1, 2026.
  • KalVista estimates that the net proceeds from the offering will be approximately $120.8M, after deducting the initial purchasers’ discount and estimated offering expenses payable by KalVista.
  • KalVista expects to use the net proceeds from the offering for working capital and other general corporate purposes, including the commercialization of EKTERLY. 
  • KalVista may also use a portion of the net proceeds from the offering for investments in and acquisitions of other companies, products, or technologies in the future.

data.title: Thursday's Economic Calendar
data.description:
8:20 AM Fed's Goolsbee Speech 
 
Chicago Federal Reserve Bank President Austan Goolsbee will participate in a moderated question-and-answer session before Crain's Power Lunch,"The Fed and the Economy: Trends for West Michigan" 
 
8:30 AM Durable Goods Orders 
 
Durable goods orders are new orders placed with domestic manufacturers for factory hard goods. 
 
Total durable goods orders are seen showing a decrease of 0.5 percent on the month in August and a decline of 0.2 percent ex-transportation orders.
 
8:30 AM GDP 
 
Gross Domestic Product represents the total value of the country's production during the period and consists of the purchases of domestically-produced goods and services by individuals, businesses, foreigners, and government entities.
 
The final revision for Q2 growth is expected to show no change from 3.3 percent in the last report.
 
8:30 AM International Trade in Goods (Advance) 
 
This monthly report offers advance import and export data on the goods components of the monthly trade report. 
 
The consensus sees the goods trade gap at $94.4 billion in August versus $103.9 billion in July.
 
8:30 AM Jobless Claims 
 
New unemployment claims are compiled weekly to show the number of individuals who filed for unemployment insurance for the first time.
 
Claims are seen at 238K in the latest week versus 231K in the prior week. 
 
8:30 AM Corporate Profits 
 
Corporate profits are derived from the national income and product accounts and are expressed in several measures. 
 
8:30 AM Retail Inventories (Advance) 
 
Retail inventories measure the monthly dollar value of inventories held by retailers.
 
8:30 AM Wholesale Inventories (Advance) 
 
Wholesale inventories measure the monthly dollar value of inventories held by merchant wholesalers and are tracked to gauge inventory change in quarterly GDP. 
 
9:00 AM Fed's Williams Speech 
 
New York Federal Reserve Bank President John Williams will give welcome remarks before the Fourth Annual International Roles of the U.S. Dollar Conference hosted by the Federal Reserve Bank of New York and the Federal Reserve Board.
 
9:00 AM Fed's Schmid Speech 
 
Kansas City Federal Reserve Bank President Jeffrey Schmid will speak on "The View from Main Street" (monetary policy, economic and banking outlook) before the Mid-Sized Bank Coalition of America.
 
10:00 AM Existing Home Sales 
 
Existing home sales tally the number of previously constructed homes, condominiums, and co-ops in which a sale closed during the month. 
 
Sales are expected to stay soft at a 3.95 million rate in August versus 4.01 million in July.
 
10:00 AM Fed's Bowman Speech
 
Federal Reserve Vice Chair for Supervision Michelle Bowman will speak on "Supervision and Regulation" before hybrid Financial Markets Quality Conference 2025.
 
10:00 AM Quarterly Services Survey 
 
The Census Bureau quarterly services survey focuses on information and technology-related service industries. 
 
10:30 AM EIA Natural Gas Report 
 
The Energy Information Administration (EIA) provides weekly information on natural gas stocks in underground storage for the U.S. and five regions of the country. The level of inventories helps determine prices for natural gas products.
 
11:00 AM Kansas City Fed Manufacturing Index 
 
The Kansas City Fed index offers a monthly assessment of change in the region's manufacturing sector. 
 
1:00 PM Fed's Barr Speech 
 
Federal Reserve Board Governor Michael Barr will participate in "Bank Stress Testing" conversation before the Peterson Institute for International Economics.
 
1:00 PM 7-Yr Note Auction 
 
The US Treasury Department will hold an auction for $44 billion of 7-year Notes.
1:40 PM Fed's Logan Speech 
 
Dallas Federal Reserve Bank President Lorie Logan Federal Reserve Bank of Dallas issues text of President Lorie Logan's remarks before closed Federal Reserve Bank of Richmond Collaboration of Research Economists Week Panel
 
3:30 PM Fed's Daly Speech 
 
San Francisco Federal Reserve Bank President Mary Daly will participate in conversation before the Federal Reserve Bank of San Francisco 2025 Western Bankers Forum.
 
4:30 PM Fed Balance Sheet 
 
The Fed's balance sheet is a weekly report presenting a consolidated balance sheet for all 12 Reserve Banks that lists factors supplying reserves into the banking system and factors absorbing reserves from the system.

data.title: KB Home outlines $1.65B Q4 housing revenue target while advancing build-to-order strategy
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: KB Home (KBH) Q3 2025

Management View

  • CEO Jeffrey Mezger reported, "We are pleased with the solid financial results that we achieved in our third quarter, meeting or exceeding our guidance ranges across our key metrics as we continue to navigate the current environment. And with a healthy balance sheet and significant cash flow, our flexibility remains strong." Mezger highlighted repurchasing more than $188 million of shares in the quarter, contributing to approximately $440 million in year-to-date repurchases, representing around 11% of the starting share count at an average price below book value.

  • Mezger emphasized operational achievements: "We had some critical achievements in terms of materially reducing our build times, which helped to generate closings that were slightly above our expectations and also continuing to lower our direct costs."

  • He noted, "We produced total revenues of over $1.6 billion and diluted earnings per share of $1.61." The company delivered a gross margin of 18.9% (excluding inventory-related charges) and held SG&A expenses to 10% of housing revenues, driving an adjusted operating income margin of 8.8%. Book value per share grew to over $60, an 11% year-over-year increase.

  • Mezger stated the intention to steer the business back to its historical mix of built-to-order homes—averaging close to 70%—from the current 50%. "As our build-to-order mix grows, we believe it will support a higher gross margin as these homes currently generate a gross margin that is 250 to 500 basis points higher than our inventory homes."

  • President & COO Rob McGibney highlighted, "We successfully opened 32 new communities during the quarter. The significant progress we made in continually reducing build times during the third quarter helped to drive better financial performance from capturing slightly more deliveries than we had planned." McGibney also noted a stable cancellation rate at 17% and an average absorption pace of 3.8 per month per community.

  • CFO Robert Dillard remarked, "In the third quarter of 2025, we generated total revenues of $1.62 billion. Housing revenues exceeded the midpoint of our guidance range at $1.61 billion, an 8% decrease from the prior year. We delivered 3,393 homes in the quarter, which exceeded the midpoint of our implied guidance, largely due to reduced build times."

Outlook

  • The company projects $1.65 billion in housing revenues for the fourth quarter of 2025 and $6.15 billion for the full fiscal year, both at the midpoints of guidance ranges. Dillard stated, "In the fourth quarter of 2025, we expect to generate housing revenues between $1.6 billion and $1.7 billion. For the full year, we now expect housing revenues between $6.1 billion and $6.2 billion."

  • Guidance includes a fourth quarter average selling price between $465,000 and $475,000, and a full-year average selling price of approximately $483,000. Dillard said, "Housing gross profit margin, assuming no inventory-related charges, is expected to be between 18% and 18.4% for the fourth quarter and between 19.2% and 19.3% for the full year."

  • The company reiterated discipline in pricing, indicating no plans to chase incremental volume in a typically slower fourth quarter.

Financial Results

  • KB Home reported total revenues of $1.62 billion for Q3 2025, with housing revenues at $1.61 billion, an 8% year-over-year decrease. The company delivered 3,393 homes in the quarter.

  • Diluted earnings per share for the quarter were $1.61. The company reported a gross margin of 18.9% (excluding inventory-related charges) and SG&A expenses at 10% of housing revenues. Adjusted operating income margin was 8.8%.

  • Net orders totaled 2,950, with a monthly absorption pace per community of 3.8 net orders.

  • The company noted a 24% reduction in ending backlog to about 4,300 homes, attributed to lower orders and improved build times.

  • Year-to-date, $490 million in capital was returned to shareholders, including $440 million in share repurchases at an average price of $56.30 per share.

Q&A

  • Stephen Kim, Evercore ISI: Asked about the sequential decline in order ASP and its implications for margins. McGibney explained that the decline is largely mix-driven, not a leading indicator for a step down in margins.

  • Kim followed up on demand trends amid declining mortgage rates. McGibney noted, "We've seen traffic stay steady. Orders have been good, but I wouldn't say that we've seen a big uptick yet...buyers are in maybe a bit of a wait-and-see mode."

  • John Lovallo, UBS: Inquired about gross margin performance and sequential changes. Dillard responded, "The real drivers there were...really strong performance on the construction side and getting the right products sold."

  • Rafe Jadrosich, BofA: Asked for preliminary 2026 outlook and BTO mix impact. Mezger stated no formal guidance for next year but expects improved affordability and higher community count, with margins expected to improve as the mix shifts to more built-to-order.

  • Alan Ratner, Zelman: Asked about progress toward increasing BTO mix and margin differentials. McGibney noted, "The margin difference is...depending on the community and the plan, it can be from 250 to 400 basis points."

  • Elizabeth Langan, Barclays: Sought detail on direct cost reductions. McGibney indicated reductions were broad-based, with particular success in lumber and leveraging market conditions with trade partners.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts displayed a neutral to slightly positive tone, focusing on margin trajectory, cost control, pricing strategy, and BTO mix. Questions reflected a search for clarity on mix impacts and forward strategy, but few signs of skepticism or deep concern.

  • Management maintained a confident and disciplined tone in both prepared remarks and Q&A, emphasizing execution, cost control, and strategic focus. Mezger stated, "We believe this is an excellent use of our cash and highly accretive to both our earnings and book value per share."

  • Compared to the previous quarter, management's tone was consistent, though confidence around operational execution and build-to-order strategy was more pronounced.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for full-year housing revenues was lowered from $6.3-6.5 billion in Q2 to $6.1-6.2 billion in Q3. Fourth quarter revenue guidance is now $1.6-1.7 billion.

  • Average selling price guidance for the full year is now approximately $483,000 versus $480,000-490,000 previously.

  • The company shifted strategic emphasis more strongly toward increasing the build-to-order share, with explicit margin benefits detailed.

  • Analysts' focus moved from questions about community opening delays and incentives to more specific concerns regarding mix, cost reductions, and margin trajectory.

  • Management's confidence in cost control and capital returns increased, highlighting year-to-date repurchases and margin preservation.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited a shortfall in net orders relative to internal goals, with absorption pace per community below recent years.

  • The company emphasized discipline in not pursuing aggressive inventory sales during the seasonally slow fourth quarter, potentially impacting volume.

  • Market volatility and buyer hesitancy tied to mortgage rate movements were noted, with some buyers "in a bit of a wait-and-see mode."

  • Land market conditions are evolving, with the company canceling contracts to purchase approximately 6,800 lots in the quarter that no longer met underwriting criteria.

  • McGibney noted regional variations in demand, with some markets like Seattle and Denver remaining challenging.

Final Takeaway

Management emphasized that KB Home delivered solid results amid a challenging environment, maintaining cost discipline and operational efficiency while accelerating its shift back toward a higher mix of built-to-order homes. The company signaled continued capital returns to shareholders and expects its focus on margin, community growth, and disciplined land strategy to support future performance, even as it navigates evolving market and demand dynamics.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Stitch Fix outlines FY '26 revenue of $1.28B–$1.33B with AI-driven personalization strategy
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Stitch Fix (SFIX) Q4 2025

Management View

  • CEO Matt Baer emphasized the company's transformation strategy, stating, "We've fundamentally reshaped how we operate by strengthening the foundation of our business and embedding retail best practices. We've also made significant strides in reimagining our client experience." He highlighted Q4 adjusted revenue growth of 4.4% to $311.2 million, noting it exceeded guidance and marked a second consecutive quarter of revenue growth. Stitch Fix gained market share in the U.S. apparel market and achieved adjusted EBITDA of $8.7 million, or 2.8% of revenue.
  • Baer described the launch of several generative AI-driven features, including an AI style assistant and a style visualization tool called Vision, both designed to personalize the shopping experience. He also announced the beta rollout of Stylist Connect, enabling direct client-stylist communication, and Family Accounts for multi-shopper convenience. The addition of over 50 new brands was noted as a driver of growth, particularly in non-apparel categories.
  • CFO David Aufderhaar stated, "We made disciplined choices to operate more efficiently, and that rigor enabled us to return to revenue growth earlier than expected, while driving significant leverage in our business." He highlighted that Stitch Fix achieved its highest contribution margin in a decade, expanded adjusted EBITDA margin by 170 basis points, and ended the year with $242.7 million in cash and no debt.

Outlook

  • For FY '26, Stitch Fix projects total revenue between $1.28 billion and $1.33 billion, with adjusted EBITDA expected between $30 million and $45 million. The company expects to remain free cash flow positive throughout the year. Q1 FY '26 revenue is forecast between $333 million and $338 million, with Q1 adjusted EBITDA between $8 million and $11 million.
  • Gross margin for FY '26 is expected to be between 43% and 44%, reflecting higher transportation costs and ongoing investments in client experience and assortment. Advertising cost is expected to be between 9% and 10% of revenue.
  • Management noted, "We expect active client year-over-year growth rates to continue to improve through the year, including a quarter-over-quarter increase in net adds."

Financial Results

  • FY '25 net revenue was $1.27 billion, with second-half revenue growing 2.5%. Gross margin for the year was 44.4%, up 10 basis points year-over-year. The company reduced SG&A spend by $124 million in FY '25.
  • Q4 net revenue was $311.2 million, up 4.4% year-over-year on an adjusted basis. Active clients ended Q4 at 2.3 million, down 7.9% year-over-year and 1.9% quarter-over-quarter. Revenue per active client increased 3% year-over-year to $549. Gross margin for Q4 was 43.6%, down 100 basis points year-over-year.
  • Q4 adjusted EBITDA was $8.7 million or 2.8% margin. Net inventory at quarter end was $118.4 million.

Q&A

  • Dana Telsey, Telsey Advisory Group: Questioned the sources of top-line growth, impact of tariffs, and holiday planning. CEO Baer explained that both women’s and men’s businesses accelerated revenue growth, driven by non-apparel and established brands. Footwear demand in women's grew over 35%, and men’s had double-digit growth, with strong demand for brands like Adidas and Marine Layer. Baer clarified, "None of the improvement in AUR, the 7.6% year-over-year growth or the growth in AOV of 12%... is attributable to tariffs."
  • Telsey followed up on market share gains and holiday outlook. Baer responded, "We continue to gain market share... our superior service is enabling us to take share from a wide variety of retailers who don't and actually cannot deliver on the personalization consumers want." He described preparations for the holiday season, highlighting flexibility, assortment improvements, and new features like Vision and Stylist Connect.
  • Aufderhaar added that trends in active clients are encouraging, with improvements in new client LTVs and recurring Fix shipments.
  • No further analyst questions were recorded.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts were positive and focused on growth drivers, market share, and the impact of tariffs and holiday season planning. Telsey acknowledged progress and sought clarity on growth composition.
  • Management maintained a confident tone, with Baer frequently expressing pride and optimism: "I'm incredibly proud of the work the Stitch Fix team has done... With this competitive edge, we're well positioned to be the retailer of choice." CFO Aufderhaar’s remarks reflected confidence in operational discipline and ongoing momentum.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, both analysts and management displayed similar optimism, with a continued emphasis on transformation and growth.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The current quarter saw explicit guidance for FY '26 revenue growth, a shift from the previous quarter's early-stage planning. Management’s strategic focus on AI, personalization, and flexible shopping options represents an evolution from prior quarters, which centered more on operational efficiency and assortment expansion.
  • Analysts’ questions shifted from client engagement and margin puts/takes in Q3 to the specific impact of new brands, tariffs, and market share strategies in Q4.
  • Key metrics such as active clients and gross margin showed sequential declines but improvement in year-over-year growth rates, signaling continued stabilization.
  • Management’s confidence remained high, with increased emphasis on technological innovation and differentiation.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management flagged a dynamic and complex macro environment entering the holiday season and cited higher transportation costs as a headwind for gross margins.
  • Tariffs were discussed as a continued risk, but mitigation efforts were credited with minimizing their impact.
  • Advertising costs are expected to rise as a percentage of revenue to support client acquisition and engagement.

Final Takeaway

Stitch Fix closed FY '25 with strong revenue growth, improved margins, and continued market share gains. The company is entering FY '26 with a focus on AI-driven personalization, expansion of flexible shopping features, and disciplined operational management. Guidance anticipates a return to full-year revenue growth, supported by new client engagement strategies, technological innovations, and a broadening assortment. Management remains confident in delivering sustainable, profitable growth despite macroeconomic uncertainties and industry headwinds.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Oil jumps by most since July as surprise drop in U.S. inventories adds to supply concerns
data.description:
Crude oil futures surged to seven-week highs Wednesday as a surprise draw in U.S. weekly crude inventories combined with continued Ukrainian strikes on Russian oil facilities to raise concerns about tightening supplies.

U.S. crude inventories fell by 607,000 barrels last week, the Energy Information Administration reported, even as imports rose and exports fell, compared to expectations for a 235,000-barrel build according to a Reuters and adding to the previous week's unexpectedly large 9.3 million barrel draw.

U.S. gasoline and distillate fuel stockpiles also fell by 1.1 million barrels and 1.7 million barrels, respectively, while domestic crude oil production added 19,000 bbl/day from the previous week to reach 13.5 million bbl/day, the EIA said.

Oil prices also were supported by news that Ukraine's military struck two oil pumping stations in Russia's Volgograd region, and a state of emergency was declared in Novorossiisk, which is Russia's major seaport on the Black Sea and contains major oil and grain export terminals.

Russia is reportedly considering restrictions on diesel exports as attacks by Ukrainian drones take a toll, while President Trump made a startling rhetorical shift, saying for the first time that Ukraine could win back all of its territory.

Traders also are said to be keeping a close eye on the latest developments regarding pipeline oil exports from Iraq's Kurdistan region to Turkey.

Front-month Nymex crude (CL1:COM) for November delivery closed +2.5% to $64.99/bbl, front-month Brent November crude also settled +2.5% to $69.31/bbl, the largest one-day percentage gain for both benchmarks since July 29, and front-month Nymex natural gas (NG1:COM) for October ended +0.2% to $2.858/MMBtu.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:USO), (BNO), (NYSEARCA:UCO), (SCO), (USL), (DBO), (DRIP), (GUSH), (USOI), (UNG), (BOIL), (KOLD), (UNL), (FCG), (NYSEARCA:XLE)

data.title: TotalEnergies to consider smaller stock buybacks in 2026
data.description:
TotalEnergies (NYSE:TTE) said Wednesday it will consider reducing the pace of its share buyback program next year, citing an uncertain economic and geopolitical environment.

TotalEnergies (NYSE:TTE) said its board has approved quarterly stock buybacks of between $750 million and $1.5 billion for FY 2026, assuming Brent crude prices at $60-$70/bbl and a dollar-euro exchange rate at ~1.2, compared with $1.5 billion in buybacks authorized for Q4 2025 - down from $2 billion in recent quarters - resulting in $7.5 billion of share buybacks for FY 2025.

The adjusted pace reflects hydrocarbon prices, refining and petrochemical margins and the U.S. dollar-to-euro exchange rate, the company said.

The board also confirmed the company's priority of preserving a strong balance sheet and retaining maneuverability by maintaining a gearing ratio below 20%, while maintaining its shareholder return policy of at least 40% of annual cash flow from operations through cycles and reaffirming the dividend as a priority in a low cycle environment.

data.title: Walker & Dunlop arranges $156M refinancing for property portfolio
data.description:
• Walker & Dunlop (NYSE:WD) announced on Wednesday that it has arranged $156.62 million to finance five multifamily communities for a portfolio located throughout the Southeast and Texas.
  • The five transactions, completed year-to-date, represent 1,351 units across four properties in the Southeast and one in Texas.
  • Walker & Dunlop Multifamily Finance, led by Allan Edelson and Joe Tarantino, arranged the transaction on behalf of long-term client, Boston Capital Real Estate Partners, with Fannie Mae providing the capital.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Aris Mining says all workers trapped at Colombia mine safely recovered
data.description:
Aris Mining (NYSE:ARMN) said Wednesday that all 23 workers who were trapped underground at the time of the La Reliquia mine shaft collapse have been safely returned to the surface.

The group included five Aris (NYSE:ARMN) employees who were conducting a routine monthly review of mine operations.

The miners were trapped for two days, but they had been receiving oxygen, food and blankets, and were in constant communication with rescuers, according to government officials.

The La Reliquia mine in Colombia is in Aris' (ARMN) land holdings but operates separately; the company said its Brigade of Prevention and Emergency Control led the rescue operation.

Aris (ARMN) shares have declined ~7% in the two days since the incident.

data.title: Air Canada sees $375M hit to Q3 operating income due to labor strikes
data.description:
• Air Canada (TSX:AC:CA) sees Q3 operated capacity declining by ~2% from the same period in 2024 as a result of the cancellation of more than 3,200 flights.
  • Operating income is like to stand between $250 million and $300 million, which includes ~$175 million from one-time non-cash pension plan amendments and other labour related charges
  • The firm sees an adjusted EBITDA within the range of $950 million and $1 billion.
  • Air Canada's operating income totaled $1.040 billion and adjusted EBITDA $1.523 billion for the third quarter of 2024.
  • The company expects to take a $375 million hit in operating income and adjusted EBITDA as an impact of labor strike.
  • Updated Full Year 2025 Outlook

    • Updated 2025 Guidance
    • Prior 2025 Guidance (Suspended on August 18, 2025)
    • Adjusted EBITDA*
    • $2.9 billion to $3.1 billion
    • $3.2 billion to $3.6 billion
    • ASM capacity
    • 0.5% to 1.5% increase versus 2024
    • 1% to 3% increase versus 2024
    • Adjusted CASM*
    • 14.60 ¢ to 14.70 ¢
    • 14.25 ¢ to 14.50 ¢
    • Free cash flow*
    • -$50 million to $150 million
    • Break even +/- $200 million
  • Q3 consensus revenue is 4.31B, EPS of $0.94.FY25 consensus revenue is 16.13B, EPS of $1.17.
  • ACDVF +0.43% after hours to $13.2871.
